industries.name: Electronics & Electrical Contacts
uses: Silver paste in silicon solar cells: silver screen-printed front contact grids collect current from the silicon wafer — each crystalline silicon solar cell uses ~80–100mg of silver paste; at 500 GW/yr global solar production, this consumes ~15% of world silver supply

Electrical contacts in switches, relays, and circuit breakers: silver's highest electrical conductivity of all metals and resistance to arc erosion make it the standard material for load-breaking contacts in everything from light switches to industrial switchgear
Multilayer ceramic capacitor (MLCC) internal electrodes: silver-palladium (Ag-Pd, 70/30 to 90/10) paste used in premium MLCCs for automotive and aerospace electronics requiring high reliability

Silver-filled conductive adhesives for die-attach and surface-mount assembly in electronics packaging — silver flake-filled epoxy bonds components to PCBs and provides both mechanical and electrical connection
RFID antennas, membrane touch switches, and flexible printed electronics use silver inkjet-printed or screen-printed conductors on plastic films and paper substrates

companies.name: Heraeus
companies.use: World's largest silver paste manufacturer for solar cells. Produces SOL9600 and Heliox series silver contact pastes for crystalline silicon solar cells. Also major silver refiner, producing silver powder, flake, and granule for electronics.

companies.name: DuPont (Photovoltaic Solutions)
companies.use: Major solar silver paste producer under the Solamet brand. Supplies silver front-contact paste to solar cell manufacturers globally. Also produces silver-based conductive materials for RFID, membrane switches, and flexible electronics.

companies.name: Fresnillo plc
companies.use: World's largest primary silver producer. Operates silver-gold mines at Fresnillo, Saucito, Herradura, and Ciénega in Mexico. Primary silver (where silver is the main revenue source, not a byproduct) is a small fraction of total supply.

industries.name: Jewelry, Silverware & Investment
uses: Sterling silver (92.5% Ag, 7.5% Cu) for jewelry, flatware, hollowware, and decorative objects — the universal standard for silver jewelry in Western markets with hallmarking requirements in most countries

Silver coins as legal tender and investment: US Silver Eagle (1 oz, 99.9% Ag), Canadian Maple Leaf, Austrian Philharmonic — government-minted bullion coins consumed 300+ million oz of silver annually at peak demand
Silver bars and rounds for retail investment: 1 oz to 1,000 oz silver bars as investment vehicles; silver ETFs (iShares Silver Trust, SLV) hold physical silver in allocated vaults
Fine silver (99.9%+) for electroplated silverware (EPNS — electroplated nickel silver) and decorative objects
Silver nitrate for mirror manufacturing: Tollen's reagent and similar silver-ammonia solutions deposit metallic silver on glass to create reflective coatings for architectural and automotive mirrors

companies.name: PAMP (MKS Group)
companies.use: World's leading silver and gold refinery and bar manufacturer. Produces PAMP Suisse silver bars and coins recognized as the global benchmark for silver investment products. Refines silver from mining and industrial sources.

companies.name: US Mint
companies.use: Produces American Silver Eagle bullion and collector coins — the world's best-selling silver investment coin. Demand from coin buyers has grown dramatically since 2020, consuming significant portions of available silver supply.

companies.name: Pandora
companies.use: World's largest jewelry brand by volume. Produces charm bracelets and jewelry primarily in sterling silver at its Bangkok manufacturing facility. Announced transition to 100% recycled silver by 2025.

industries.name: Industrial & Photography
uses: Silver brazing alloys (Ag-Cu-Zn-Cd or Ag-Cu-In): used to join dissimilar metals in HVAC refrigerant circuits, electrical equipment, and aerospace components where high-strength, corrosion-resistant joints are required at moderate temperatures
Silver-oxide and silver-zinc batteries for hearing aids, watches, and military/aerospace applications where high energy density and flat discharge voltage are more important than cost

Water purification: silver-impregnated activated carbon filters and silver-coated ceramic filter elements in point-of-use water purifiers in developing markets — silver's broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity prevents bacterial regrowth

Silver halide photographic film and paper: still produced for artistic, motion picture (Kodak Vision3), and archival use — a small fraction of peak demand but silver sensitivity remains unmatched for certain applications

Silver catalysts for ethylene oxide (EO) production: silver supported on alpha-alumina at 200–300°C selectively oxidizes ethylene to ethylene oxide (EO) — EO is converted to ethylene glycol for PET plastic and antifreeze, and to surfactants

companies.name: Eastman Kodak
companies.use: Produces Kodak Vision3 motion picture film and TMAX/Tri-X professional photographic films using silver halide emulsions. Also produces X-ray film for industrial NDT. A fraction of peak silver demand but still significant.

companies.name: Shell Catalysts & Technologies
companies.use: Major supplier of silver ethylene oxide (EO) catalysts to chemical plants globally. Its METEOR and CRS series silver/α-Al₂O₃ catalysts are used to produce EO for ethylene glycol (antifreeze, PET polyester) and surfactant production.

companies.name: Lucas-Milhaupt (Handy & Harman)
companies.share: ~15% of North American silver brazing alloy market
companies.use: Major silver brazing alloy manufacturer. Produces Sil-Fos, Safety-Silv, and other Ag-Cu-P and Ag-Cu-Zn brazing filler metals for HVAC, refrigeration, and industrial metal joining applications.

industries.name: Antimicrobial & Medical
uses: Silver sulfadiazine cream (1% AgSD) as the standard topical treatment for burn wound infection — silver ions kill gram-positive and gram-negative bacteria and Pseudomonas aeruginosa in burn eschar

Silver-coated wound dressings (Aquacel Ag, Mepilex Ag, Acticoat) for chronic wounds, diabetic foot ulcers, and surgical site infections — sustained silver ion release from dressing maintains antimicrobial activity for 7 days

Nanosilver particles in textiles (athletic wear, socks, underwear) for odor control by inhibiting odor-causing bacteria — regulatory scrutiny increasing due to nanosilver environmental persistence and aquatic toxicity
Silver-coated urinary catheters and endotracheal tubes to reduce catheter-associated urinary tract infections (CAUTI) and ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) in hospital settings
Silver-impregnated bone cement and implant coatings for infection prevention in revision joint replacement surgery where infection rates are high

companies.name: Smith & Nephew
companies.use: Major advanced wound care company producing ACTICOAT silver-coated wound dressings and ALLEVYN Ag foam dressings. Silver-based antimicrobial wound products are a core part of its wound management portfolio for burns and chronic wounds.

companies.name: Mölnlycke Health Care
companies.use: Produces Mepilex Border Ag and Mepitel Ag silver-containing wound dressings for infection prevention in surgical wounds, pressure ulcers, and traumatic wounds. Major supplier to hospital wound care teams globally.
